channel.title: RPI3 on Artoo.ai
channel.description: Recent content in RPI3 on Artoo.ai

item.title: Install ROS2 On Raspberry Pi 3 (RPi3)
item.description: I had issues getting ROS2 on to RPi3. It all revolved around ArmV7 vs ARM64. All the linux distributions need to ARM64. It also has to be the latest version of Ubuntu. I tried many ways to get the latest version of Ubuntu (18.04), but then when using “apt” to install ROS2, or build ROS2 from source I always still got an error. I then saw that RPi3 not supports Ubuntu RPi3 Server edition.

item.title: R2D2 Arm Connected to RPI3 With PCA9685
item.description: RPI3 You can use the RPI3 to use the servos. But the servo power will need to separte from the RPI3. The RPI3’s 5V is not enough to use servos long term. For initial testing they will work. But what you will find is the servos will glitch if connected to the RPI3’s header. The RPI3 causes the glitches. So eventually you need to find an alternative way to run the servos.

item.title: R2D2 Arm Connected to RPI3 With Micro Maestro
item.description: RPI3 You can use the RPI3 to use the servos. But the servo power will need to separte from the RPI3. The RPI3’s 5V is not enough to use servos long term. For initial testing they will work. But what you will find is the servos will glitch if connected to the RPI3’s header. The RPI3 causes the glitches. So eventually you need to find an alternative way to run the servos.

item.title: R2D2 Arm Connected to RPI3
item.description: RPI3 You can use the RPI3 to use the servos. But the servo power will need to separte from the RPI3. The RPI3’s 5V is not enough to use servos long term. For initial testing they will work. But what you will find is the servos will glitch if connected to the RPI3’s header. The RPI3 causes the glitches. So eventually you need to find an alternative way to run the servos.

item.title: R2D2 Flask GUI
item.description: Flask GUI The code is written in Python so I can utilize ROS in the future. I wanted to use a GUI that I can use in the web browser, so it will work on any platform including mobile. The RPI3 in my R2D2 will have an wifi connection. So I can connect Ad-Hoc directly to the RPI3 and use the browser to control some of the accessories in my R2D2.
